key_insight: VRR-Stop decides when to stop verify-repair loops by estimating whether the next repair is likely to improve true validity, while remaining robust to noisy verifiers and repairers.
engineering_relevance: This gives LLM-agent pipelines a principled stopping rule that can reduce wasted repair rounds and avoid over-repair that damages already-correct solutions.
method: Models verifier and repair noise with four parameters: false acceptance, false rejection, repair improvement, and repair damage.
Uses belief filtering over repeated verification votes to estimate committed validity and compare the true marginal gain of repairing versus stopping.
Adds VRR-Guard as a fallback when verifier discrimination is too weak for reliable calibration.
evidence_signal: On a GSM8K stress setting, VRR-Stop improved final true validity by 60.6 percentage points over fixed five-round repair with an average of 0.72 repair rounds.
watch_item: The stopping rule depends on verifier discrimination and a sufficient decision margin; if verifier separation is near zero, calibration can fail and the fallback guard may be needed.
paper_title: Verify, Repair, Repeat, or Stop? Robust Stopping for Noisy Verify-Repair Loops in LLM Agents
